issues: 1d trend labeled bearish/strong while EMA slope is +3.41 with higher highs and higher lows — structural contradiction never resolved, just flagged
Volume anomaly (1h 7%, 4h 2% of MA) is severe enough to question all directional conviction, yet system still reaffirmed SHORT with confidence 7
Hash rate 7d and 30d averages identical at 940.2 EH/s with 0.0% trend despite 26.5% spot spike — stale data pipeline likely
Macro analyst tools still missing (recurring issue since Mar 4) — macro data retrieved via workarounds but this is a persistent gap
FM decision block says ACTION: HOLD but preamble says FINAL TRANSACTION PROPOSAL: SHORT — mixed signals, though executor correctly interpreted as HOLD
improvements: Fix the 1d trend direction logic — when higher_highs AND higher_lows are true with positive EMA slope, the trend cannot be bearish/strong. This mislabel cascades into every downstream agent
Confidence should be capped at 5 when volume is below 10% of MA on multiple timeframes — the system lacks a hard rule for conviction-killing data quality
Resolve the macro_tools module gap permanently — it's been flagged for 2 consecutive days with no fix, forcing workarounds every run
FM output format needs enforcing — ACTION field and preamble text must agree to avoid ambiguity
summary: Solid decision chain that correctly held an existing short on a low-conviction bear rally. The debate was substantive — the aggressive risk analyst's 'coiled spring' volume argument genuinely stress-tested the thesis. Main weakness is the unresolved 1d trend/structure contradiction that's been propagating bad labels into agent reasoning for multiple runs, plus the persistent macro_tools module gap degrading data coverage.
